_comment: data/horologion/vespers-kathisma.json — Horologion v1.5
PURPOSE: Appointment table for the Kathisma reading at Vespers.
Keyed by weekday (Sunday–Saturday) for ordinary non-festal Vespers.
RULE: Standard Byzantine Psalter weekly cycle for Vespers.
SOURCE: OCA/Antiochian Psalter table; Jordanville Horologion (2008).
THIS FILE PROVIDES: Kathisma number, title, psalm citation (LXX and
Hebrew/Protestant), and the opening incipit line. It does NOT
contain full psalm text. The engine renders a rubric identifying
the appointed kathisma; the text is read from a Psalter.
WHY NO FULL TEXT: Each kathisma spans 8–10+ psalms. Embedding full
text for 7 kathismata inline is a separate data-import task.
This baseline resolves the 'what is appointed' question with an
honest, useful output. Full text population is deferred.
OVERRIDES NOT IMPLEMENTED (explicitly noted, not silently absent):
- Great Feasts: kathisma typically omitted or replaced by the feast
- Great Lent: entire Psalter read twice weekly (different distribution)
- Bright Week (Pascha–Thomas Saturday): no kathisma
- Sunday: practice varies by usage; explicitly deferred
- Feast-rank engine: deferred to a future pass
WEEKDAY INDEX: JS Date.getDay() — 0=Sunday, 1=Monday, 6=Saturday.

meta.numbering: LXX (Septuagint) psalm numbers used throughout, as per Byzantine liturgical practice

assignments.note: Sunday Vespers kathisma practice varies significantly by usage. At full Great Vespers (all-night vigil), Kathisma 1 is standard. At ordinary Sunday small Vespers, the kathisma is often omitted or differs by local use. Sunday Vespers kathisma is explicitly deferred in this baseline. The appointed kathisma number is not fabricated.

assignments.title: Fourth Kathisma
assignments.incipit: To Thee, O Lord, have I lifted up my soul

assignments.title: Sixth Kathisma
assignments.incipit: Rebuke me not, O Lord, in Thine anger

assignments.title: Eighth Kathisma
assignments.incipit: Have mercy on me, O God, for man hath trodden me down

assignments.title: Tenth Kathisma
assignments.incipit: In Thee, O Lord, have I hoped

assignments.title: Twelfth Kathisma
assignments.incipit: Incline Thine ear, O Lord, and hear me
assignments.variant_note: Some editions (Jordanville) include Psalm 91 (LXX) / Psalm 92 (Protestant) within this kathisma; others end at Psalm 90. The citation above follows the standard OCA weekday Vespers Psalter table.

assignments.title: Blessed is the Man
assignments.incipit: Blessed is the man who walks not in the counsel of the ungodly
assignments.rubric: Kathisma 1 — Blessed is the Man (Psalms 1–8 LXX)
